updates.tweet_text: The hardest part of trading isnt trading, its waiting. The market will present at least 1 high win rate, planned, A+ setup daily. But you have to wait for it, and not take all the 50/50 boredom trades that will eat the profit from the A+ trade before it even hits. Less=more
updates.notes: Pure trading philosophy/mindset commentary. No prices mentioned, no actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text:
Huge follow through in #ES_F: Last Wednesday we got long on the 7311 Failed Breakdown. All week, I had one target I repeated 5x times daily: 7590. We finally hit last eve, for +279 points.

Plan today: Work done, ride runner. 7611, 7620, 7639 bonus. 7590, 7570=micro supports https://t.co/lKiQdxpBbb
updates.notes: All prices (7311, 7590, 7611, 7639, 7570) are morning-plan levels or hindsight references. 7620 appears in a directional list of existing targets, no new naming language.

updates.tweet_text: @livezey954181 They're not supports I'd engage but I still throw them out there for people who may want to. I only trade if we get a deep elevator down sell and failed breakdown. Once we get volatility I start posting actual entries not just supporrts

updates.tweet_text: @atlasbtcgrowth A dip is always healthy/bullish. Have to thing the opposite of what retail traders think. When they see red they think its bearish and the market is topping, but the reality is more red in an uptrend=healthier and more continued upside later
updates.notes: Pure educational/philosophical commentary on market psychology. No price levels named, no actionable entries, targets, or triggers mentioned.
